speaker: Donald J. Trump
quote: Well, it's not going to be that much different from what I did last night. I mean, we've had tremendous success. We're bringing prices down, we inherited a mess and part of what we inherited was the worst inflation in 48 years, I say history. But we had the worst inflation, it drove prices up and now we're bringing those prices down.
venue: Remarks: Donald Trump Signs an Executive Order to Ease Cannibis Rules
claim_text: Trump said Biden-era inflation was the highest/worst in U.S. history.

sources.publisher: Federal Reserve FRED CPIAUCSL (BLS source series)
Federal Reserve FRED CPIAUCSL CSV (BLS source)

assessment.rationale:
Evidence:
- The transcript quote uses an absolute historical superlative for inflation.
- The CPIAUCSL series (BLS source, published via Federal Reserve FRED) shows a higher inflation peak in 1980 than in the 2021-2022 period.
- The Biden-era surge was severe, but not the highest value in the official long-run CPI record.

Why This Is False:
- "Highest in history" is a numeric ranking claim, not a subjective description.
- Official CPI history places earlier inflation peaks above the Biden-era peak.
- That makes the historical ranking in the claim incorrect.

Shut Down False Argument:
- Saying inflation was painful does not prove it was the all-time maximum.
- The same official CPI dataset used to discuss modern inflation also records higher historical peaks.
- On that evidence standard, the claim fails.
